digest.greeting: Thursday, July 2, Jean. Today is anchored by EPMO EOW, the Amazon Connect delay review, the EPMO intake/BA process conversation, and the afternoon portfolio/SMS/Tudo Bem meeting run. Outlook shows 10 calendar blocks, 0 urgent carried-over items, 0 active reminders, and no unchecked carry-over from the prior daily digest based on GitHub taskId state. Keep the day centered on preparing the programmatic update path, making the Amazon Connect delay summary decision-ready for Danny and Jeff, and using the BA/intake meeting to land a clean process flow with Anu and Janet. Direct Teams reads were rate-limited this morning, so Teams is represented only where Outlook surfaced Teams notifications.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times are shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day starts with executive/programmatic context, has a short late-morning onboarding/process cluster, then runs steadily from 1:30 PM through Tudo Bem at 4:00 PM.

items.label: EPMO EOW Check In
items.detail: Moved one hour earlier and expanded to include Nadia for programmatic updates. Use it to align what needs to be escalated, summarized, or carried into Friday.

items.label: Amazon Connect - Delay's Overview
items.detail: Nadia scheduled this to make sure Danny and Jeff have a summary that answers Jon's ask on Amazon Connect and Agentforce Voice delays. The attached executive summary and supporting artifact are the prep materials.

items.label: Meet & greet with Armando and Astrid
items.detail: Short meet-and-greet block for Armando, Astrid, Jean, and Janet. Keep it contained so it does not crowd the BA/process meeting that follows.

items.label: EPMO intake - BA request process
items.detail: Process-alignment meeting with Janet, Jorge, Anu, Jean, and Eugenia optional. Agenda is BA-request workflow for department and enterprise projects plus a bi-weekly IT prioritization touchpoint.

items.label: Amazon Connect weekly project sync
items.detail: Use only the remaining decision points from the delay overview; avoid re-opening background unless it changes the delay narrative or next owner.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best protected window for writing down decisions from the EOW, Amazon Connect, and BA-process meetings before the afternoon stack starts.

items.label: 1:1 Thiago and Jean
items.detail: Useful transition and structure-change follow-up slot before portfolio review.

items.label: Mkt / Legal Portfolio Review
items.detail: Portfolio review with Eugenia and Jean on the invite. Tie it back to any open TRACE, marketing/legal, or portfolio-routing decisions rather than treating it as a standalone update.

items.label: SMS Automation Optimization Weekly Project Status
items.detail: Agenda explicitly includes progress, priorities, resources, risks, blockers, escalations, and next steps. Bring any open escalation or owner question to the surface.

items.label: Tudo Bem!'s Weekly Meeting
items.detail: Main team coordination forum for progress, roadblocks, and strategic initiative momentum. Use it to close the loop on anything surfaced during the afternoon stack.

items.label: Prepare the EOW programmatic update path
items.detail: The EOW check-in was moved earlier and expanded to include Nadia. Go in with the few updates that need leadership attention, especially anything tied to Amazon Connect, intake routing, or portfolio review.

items.label: Make the Amazon Connect delay summary decision-ready
items.detail: Nadia's meeting invite says the goal is to confirm whether the executive summary and supporting artifact cover Jon's ask on project delays. Review the materials before 10:00 AM and be ready to flag gaps, owner questions, or missing timeline context for Danny and Jeff.

items.label: Land the EPMO intake and BA-request process
items.detail: Use the Anu/Janet/Jorge meeting to clarify how department and enterprise projects request BA support, and whether the bi-weekly IT prioritization call becomes the operating cadence for priority shifts.

items.label: Bring clear blockers to SMS Automation status
items.detail: The SMS Automation agenda is built for decisions: progress, priorities, resources, risks, blockers, escalations, and next steps. Prepare any unresolved owner or resource question before the 2:30 PM meeting.

items.label: Check the dashboard PR #7 preview only if it affects visibility
items.detail: An unread GitHub/Vercel notification says the pmdashboard PR #7 preview is ready for the Planner task queue snapshot. Treat this as a quick verification item if Jean needs confidence that the dashboard task visibility work is behaving before it merges.
